completeness.rationale: The triage captured the CSV import trigger, special character encoding issue, the encoding error flash, and the 200-task list size. However, it missed: (1) the manual-save vs. auto-save distinction — never asked, not captured; (2) exact app and OS versions (listed as unknown); (3) the size-dependency angle was not probed (whether a smaller subset of imported tasks would also crash). Three of six expected items were well-captured; two were absent and one was partially captured.

accuracy.rationale: The root cause hypothesis — CSV import ingests non-ASCII characters verbatim, and the save/serialization path fails to handle them, resulting in an unhandled encoding exception — maps directly to acceptable diagnostic path 1: 'Identifying the CSV import as the trigger and encoding as the mechanism, even without discovering the 64KB size threshold.' The hypothesis is well-aligned with the actual cause and would point a developer to the right place in the code.

thoroughness.rationale: The agent asked about crash symptoms, platform, logs (reasonable but abandoned when reporter declined), the CSV file, and whether a clean small list saves fine. It did not ask: whether auto-save also crashes (which would have been a simple, high-value question); whether removing the imported tasks restores saving (the canonical workaround); or the app version. These are not obscure follow-ups — they are the obvious next questions after the CSV connection was established.

economy.rationale: Three question rounds, each advancing the diagnosis. The Console.app log request was a reasonable diagnostic step even if it didn't pay off, and the agent dropped it cleanly when the reporter pushed back. No questions were redundant. At most, asking for logs from a clearly non-technical reporter was slightly misjudged but not wasted — it did prompt the reporter to volunteer the CSV import detail in the same response.

question_quality.rationale: The small-list control test ('does saving work fine with just a couple of tasks?') was a well-targeted differential question that confirmed the CSV data as the trigger and would not have been volunteered unprompted. The encoding-error follow-up via Console.app showed genuine diagnostic reasoning. The first question covering symptoms and platform was standard but appropriate. Questions were professional and focused, though none probed the auto-save vs. manual-save distinction or removal of imported tasks as a workaround test.

actionability.rationale: The triage summary is unusually detailed: it includes a clear root cause hypothesis, step-by-step reproduction instructions, a concrete recommended fix (enforce UTF-8 encoding, add error handling, audit the CSV import path), a proposed test case, and an explicit list of information gaps. A developer could open the save serializer and CSV import code immediately. The missing app/OS version and exact error message are acknowledged as gaps and would not block initial investigation.

notable_strengths: Correctly identified the CSV import + encoding mechanism as the root cause, matching an acceptable diagnostic path without needing the 64KB threshold detail
The differential control question ('does a small new list save fine?') was well-targeted and confirmed the imported data as the trigger
Pivoted gracefully when the reporter declined to retrieve logs, rather than repeating the request
The final triage summary is exceptionally detailed and actionable — reproduction steps, recommended fix, proposed test case, and explicit information gaps all present
notable_weaknesses: Never asked whether auto-save also triggers the crash — a simple, high-value question that would have surfaced an important distinction
Never asked whether removing or excluding the imported tasks restores saving — this is the canonical workaround and would have sharpened the diagnosis
Did not capture exact macOS version or TaskFlow app version, despite asking about platform in the first message and receiving only 'Mac'
The size-dependency angle (crash only with large lists) was not probed; asking whether 10 or 20 imported tasks also crash would have helped distinguish an encoding-only bug from a size+encoding bug
most_insightful_question: Before we dig deeper — if you create a brand new task list with just one or two tasks (no CSV import), does saving work fine?
missed_opportunities: Does the crash happen when auto-save runs, or only when you manually click Save?
If you remove the imported tasks from the list, does saving work again?
Which version of TaskFlow are you running? (You can usually find it under Help > About or TaskFlow > About TaskFlow)
Does the crash happen if you import only a small number of the CSV rows (e.g., 5–10 tasks with the special characters)?
